Speculative Connections Between Gaumata, Gautama Buddha, and Mithras

Absolutely! Here’s a table timeline that not only summarizes the political events from your text, but also highlights how this period helped lay the groundwork for the later spread of Mithraism, Maitreyaism, Buddhism, and the archetype of Eurasian messianic/solar figures. Where possible, I’ll add relevant connections and scholarly context.
Mithraism: Emerges from Persian worship of Mithra, god of contracts and light; becomes a Roman mystery religion with a solar, savior-like figure.
Maitreyaism: The prophecy of a future Buddha (Maitreya) who will restore order and enlightenment echoes the Zoroastrian Saoshyant and the Persian “world restorer” motif.
Buddhism: Spreads along Persian trade routes; Buddhist kingship and messianic ideas are influenced by Persian imperial ideology and Zoroastrian eschatology.

Messianic Archetypes: The crisis of 522 BCE (with its themes of hidden kings, usurpation, and righteous restoration) provides a narrative template for later Eurasian messianic figures, including those associated with solar deities (Mithra, Apollo, Sol Invictus, Dionysus).
Plutarch, Life of Pompey (on Mithraic mysteries)
Scholarly works: Mary Boyce, Zoroastrians; Richard Foltz, Religions of the Silk Road; David Ulansey, The Origins of the Mithraic Mysteries

The succession crisis and imperial reforms of the Achaemenid era (especially under Darius I) created the conditions for religious syncretism, the spread of salvationist and solar cults, and the enduring Eurasian archetype of the hidden or returning savior-a template that would influence Mithraism, Maitreyaism, Buddhism, and the solar deities of the Greco-Roman world.
